name: Peñas de Sala Trail
name_original: Ruta Peñas de Sala — circular desde Teresa de Cofrentes
description: Hike the moderate 5.9-mile (9.4 km) Route Peñas de Sala circular from Teresa de Cofrentes, featuring impressive views and pine forests.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Teresa De Cofrentes traverse a landscape characterized by river valleys, rolling hills, and Mediterranean forests. The region features varied terrain, including areas with water features and rocky outcrops. Elevations are generally moderate, making it accessible for different hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Teresa De Cofrentes The most popular hiking route is Hoz de Zarra Trail, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores a notable ravine landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ruta del Agua — circular in Ayora, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This circular route features water elements and is suitable for a shorter outing. Local hikers also love the Peñas de Sala Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.4 km) trail leading through areas with rocky formations, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Teresa De Cofrentes is defined by river valleys, moderate hills, and Mediterranean woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Teresa De Cofrentes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. Nearly 1,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Teresa De Cofrentes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Teresa De Cofrentes?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails in the Teresa De Cofrentes region, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Teresa De Cofrentes offers 21 easy hiking routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1099725322/ruta-del-agua-circular-in-ayora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Agua — circular in Ayora&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.3 miles (6.9 km) long and features water elements, making it ideal for a shorter, pleasant outing.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Teresa De Cofrentes?
A: The hiking trails around Teresa De Cofrentes traverse a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, rolling hills, and Mediterranean forests. You'll encounter varied terrain, including areas with water features and rocky outcrops, with generally moderate elevations.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. Besides the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1099725322/ruta-del-agua-circular-in-ayora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta del Agua — circular in Ayora&lt;/a&gt;, you can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1098287965/route-of-montemayor-circular-from-ayora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of Montemayor — circular from Ayora&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail.

Q: What are some challenging hiking trails in Teresa De Cofrentes?
A: For those seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult hiking routes in the region. These trails typically feature more significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, providing a more strenuous experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Teresa De Cofrentes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 130 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to rocky formations, and the well-maintained paths that offer diverse experiences.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or wild swimming spots along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region features several water attractions. You can find waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3216823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de Cortes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. For a potential wild swimming spot, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de La Tosca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I explore near the trails?
A: Beyond the trails, the region is known for its caves and historical sites. You can visit natural formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4593119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tejador Cave&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5731664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Don Juan&lt;/a&gt;. There's also the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4600612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calicanto raft shelter&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the area's history.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Teresa De Cofrentes?
A: Given its Mediterranean climate, the best times for hiking in Teresa De Cofrentes are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are mild, and the natural scenery is vibrant, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any shorter, easy walks for a quick outing?
A: Absolutely. For a quick and easy walk, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101268127/the-route-of-the-red-thread-circular-in-ayora" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Route of the Red Thread — circular in Ayora&lt;/a&gt;. This route is just under 2 miles (3.2 km) and can be completed in about an hour, perfect for a leisurely stroll.

Q: Is public transport available to access the hiking trailheads?
A: Public transport options directly to many trailheads in the rural areas around Teresa De Cofrentes are limited. It is generally recommended to use a private vehicle to reach the starting points of most hikes.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Teresa De Cofrentes, especially in spring?
A: In spring, temperatures can vary, so layered clothing is advisable. Wear comfortable, moisture-wicking base layers, a light jacket, and sturdy hiking shoes with good grip. Don't forget sun protection like a hat and sunscreen, as the sun can be strong even in spring.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot data suggests this is a moderate hike that typically takes about 2 hours and 34 minutes to complete. However, local tourism information from Teresa de Cofrentes categorizes it as 'easy' and estimates a duration of 3 hours. Your actual time may vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally characterized by easily-accessible paths. While specific details for this route are limited, the surrounding area features varied landscapes including pine forests and soft rock formations, which have created ravines and gorges. Expect a mix of these natural elements.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Hikers can enjoy impressive views of the Peñas de Sala, a significant natural monument. The route also features natural flora and fauna, poplars (chopos) along the riverside, and wide-angle views of valleys and a 'garrigue' landscape. Specific highlights include Fuente Velilla, the Cautabán River Crossing, and a Forest Path Along the Riverside.

faqs.question: Is the Route Peñas de Sala — circular from Teresa de Cofrentes dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail offers natural beauty, specific regulations regarding dogs are not provided in the available information. It's always best to check with local authorities or visitor centers in Teresa de Cofrentes for the most current rules on bringing dogs, especially regarding leash requirements and waste disposal.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for hiking in this region, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be hot, and winter conditions might vary, so check the forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on the available information, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to access the Route Peñas de Sala — circular from Teresa de Cofrentes. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Teresa de Cofrentes. While specific parking locations are not detailed, it is generally understood that parking is available within the town of Teresa de Cofrentes, allowing access to the trailhead. Look for public parking areas upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this as a 'moderate' hike requiring 'good fitness', while local tourism describes it as 'easy'. Given its circular nature and 'easily-accessible paths', it can be suitable for beginners with a reasonable level of fitness and families who are accustomed to walking for a few hours. Always assess your group's capabilities before starting.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Los Castillos del Valle trail, covering approximately 32% of its length.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead in Teresa de Cofrentes?
faqs.answer: Teresa de Cofrentes is a local town, and you can expect to find basic amenities such as cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refresh yourself before or after your hike. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are advisable. A map or GPS device (like the Komoot app) is also useful for navigation.
